overview.short: Bangalore Diocese is one of the 32 diocese of the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church also known as Indian Orthodox Church located in Bangalore, Karnataka.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jalahalli
topPlaces.description: Jalahalli a suburb in northern part of Bengaluru is one of the greenest areas of Bengaluru. It is mainly divided into Jalahalli East and Jalahalli West.

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya, Jalahalli West
topPlaces.description: Kendriya Vidyalaya, No.1, Air Force Station, Jalahalli West, Bengaluru, India is a school run by the Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan, an autonomous body formed by the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India, New Delhi. Started in 1964, the Vidyalaya has classes from I to XII with an enrollment of 2300 with Science and Commerce streams at the Plus-Two level.

topPlaces.name: Jalahalli inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: Jalahalli inscriptions and hero stones are relics found in a microlithic tool factory in Jalahalli, a locality in North Bengaluru in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Vidyaranyapura
topPlaces.description: Vidyaranyapura is a residential locality situated in the northern part of Bangalore, in the state of Karnataka, India. Along with several other extensions, Vidyaranyapura falls under the Byatarayanapura constituency.

topPlaces.name: Singapura inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: Singapura is a historic locality in northwestern Bengaluru, with evidence indicating human habitation dating back to 2,500 to 3,000 years. Singapura is renowned for the Varadarajaswamy Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kodigehalli
topPlaces.description: Kodigehalli, one of Bengaluru's oldest villages, is now included in BBMP Ward 8 under the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP). A blend of history and modernity, Kodigehalli is home to ancient temples and modern infrastructure, playing a significant role in Bengaluru's rapidly developing northern corridor.

topPlaces.name: Thindlu
topPlaces.description: Thindlu, once an ancient village, is now a part of the Bangalore North. Thindlu is famous for its ancient temple of lord Virabhadra (a form lord Shiva) and is currently a thriving community with many real estate constructions.

topPlaces.name: Kodigehalli inscriptions and hero stones
topPlaces.description: The area encompassing Kodigehalli and its sub-localities—Tindlu and Doddabommasandra—in Bengaluru contains several epigraphical and sculptural artifacts dating primarily from the 14th to 16th centuries CE. These include three Kannada inscriptions and two hero stones (Viragal, commemorative stones for fallen warriors). The name "Kodigehalli" likely originates from the Kannada words kodige (grant) and halli (village), possibly referencing a land grant detailed in one of the inscriptions found here.

items.note: Jalahalli a suburb in northern part of Bengaluru is one of the greenest areas of Bengaluru. It is mainly divided into Jalahalli East and Jalahalli West.

Kendriya Vidyalaya, No.1, Air Force Station, Jalahalli West, Bengaluru, India is a school run by the Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan, an autonomous body formed by the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India, New Delhi. Started in 1964, the Vidyalaya has classes from I to XII with an enrollment of 2300 with Science and Commerce streams at the Plus-Two level.
Jalahalli inscriptions and hero stones are relics found in a microlithic tool factory in Jalahalli, a locality in North Bengaluru in the Indian state of Karnataka.

Vidyaranyapura is a residential locality situated in the northern part of Bangalore, in the state of Karnataka, India. Along with several other extensions, Vidyaranyapura falls under the Byatarayanapura constituency.
Singapura is a historic locality in northwestern Bengaluru, with evidence indicating human habitation dating back to 2,500 to 3,000 years. Singapura is renowned for the Varadarajaswamy Temple.

Kodigehalli, one of Bengaluru's oldest villages, is now included in BBMP Ward 8 under the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP). A blend of history and modernity, Kodigehalli is home to ancient temples and modern infrastructure, playing a significant role in Bengaluru's rapidly developing northern corridor.

Thindlu, once an ancient village, is now a part of the Bangalore North. Thindlu is famous for its ancient temple of lord Virabhadra (a form lord Shiva) and is currently a thriving community with many real estate constructions.

The area encompassing Kodigehalli and its sub-localities—Tindlu and Doddabommasandra—in Bengaluru contains several epigraphical and sculptural artifacts dating primarily from the 14th to 16th centuries CE. These include three Kannada inscriptions and two hero stones (Viragal, commemorative stones for fallen warriors). The name "Kodigehalli" likely originates from the Kannada words kodige (grant) and halli (village), possibly referencing a land grant detailed in one of the inscriptions found here.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (23 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally?
faq.a: Stays in St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Mary's Orthodox Valiyapally?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jalahalli, Kendriya Vidyalaya, Jalahalli West, Jalahalli inscriptions and hero stones, Vidyaranyapura, Singapura inscriptions and hero stones.
